title: Tall Bench Seat with Footrest
summary: A bar-height bench with an angled backrest and a built-in low footrest bar between the legs — bench-height seating for a tall table or counter.
description:
This bench pairs with counter- or bar-height tables by building the seat itself tall and giving it a dedicated footrest bar, since a standard-height bench under a tall table leaves legs dangling uncomfortably. Build the seat frame first, then attach the legs to its outside with screws driven at a slight angle — that angle is deliberate, giving the joint more resistance to the sideways racking a tall, narrow bench takes on regularly.

Once the legs are on, flip the whole assembly over to attach the side leg supports, checking that the legs sit flat against the tabletop frame before fastening — a leg that's slightly proud at this stage throws the whole bench off level permanently. The back support goes in between the back legs, and the back cross supports follow, both adding real rigidity to what would otherwise be a tall, top-heavy frame.

The footrest support attaches low between the front legs, and the footrest boards go on last, laid across it the same way the seat boards go on the top — a small but genuinely useful detail for anyone sitting at this height for more than a few minutes.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 15° off square, long point to short point, ends parallel.
One end cut at 30° off square, long point measurement.
Both ends cut at 30° off square, long point to short point, ends parallel.

steps.title: Build the seat frame
steps.body: Build the seat frame from the sides and ends with two screws per joint.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the back and front legs to the outside of the seat frame with screws driven at a slight angle into the legs.

steps.title: Add the back support
steps.body: Attach the back support between the back legs.

steps.title: Attach the side leg supports
steps.body: Turn the bench over and attach the side leg supports, making sure the legs sit flat against the tabletop frame.

steps.title: Add the footrest support and back cross supports
steps.body: Attach the footrest support between the front legs with screws. Attach the back cross supports.

steps.title: Add the seat and footrest boards, then finish
steps.body: Attach the seat boards on top of the frame and the footrest boards on the footrest support. Sand and finish.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Tall bar-height bench with a built-in footrest bar
